$note: Trackable manual patches to the Tier 1 countries catalog. Applied at Tier 2 build time AFTER loading Tier 1 src-data and BEFORE cross-catalog M:M backfill. Each entry carries a `reason` + `addedAt` so the policy decision is audit-trail visible without git-archaeology. Use `pnpm geo:overlays` to list active patches with their reasons. See ./README.md for the pattern + when to add an overlay vs fix upstream.

additions.reason: Kosovo declared independence 2008; recognized by ~100 UN members but blocked from full UN membership by Russia/China veto. ISO 3166-1 has not officially assigned a code; XK is the user-assigned code adopted by EU institutions, IMF, SWIFT, IBAN registry, Visa/Mastercard, and most commercial systems. Datasets/country-codes (our Tier 1 upstream) conservatively excludes XK because there's no official ISO assignment. Adding here because (a) the hand-rolled GeopoliticalEntity catalog references XK as a member of `EU` (Europe continent) + `BALK` (Balkans region) so the GE→Country FK would be orphan without this addition, and (b) downstream consumers using the geo catalogs for address forms / locale resolution / etc. realistically need Kosovo. Alpha-3 + numeric codes are user-assigned per ISO 3166-1 rules (XKK / 983 are the most widely-used unofficial assignments — EU and IBAN registries use these).

overrides.reason: Argentina uses USD as de-facto secondary currency widely accepted at retail; CLDR's activeLegalTenderCurrencies (ARS only) is upstream-correct since USD is not legal tender, but downstream consumers need USD acceptance flagged for FX / checkout / payment-method-selection flows.

Switzerland accepts EUR widely at retail and tourism venues alongside CHF; CHF remains sole legal tender so upstream Tier 1 is correct, but cross-border commerce and tourism justify flagging EUR acceptance for downstream consumers.

Lebanon is de-facto dual-currency (LBP + USD) due to the ongoing currency crisis since 2019; USD is widely accepted at retail and increasingly required for many transactions, despite LBP being sole legal tender per upstream Tier 1.

Hong Kong widely accepts CNY alongside HKD due to mainland China cross-border commerce and tourism volumes; CNY is not legal tender (HKD is sole legal tender) so flagging via WidelyAccepted is the correct modeling.

Cambodia is de-facto dual-currency (KHR + USD) with USD widely accepted at retail and frequently the preferred currency for larger transactions, despite KHR being sole legal tender per upstream Tier 1.
